t_ch: 最后是下层区的势力——「地火」组织。
t_en: Lastly, we have the main strength of the Underworld — Wildfire.

t_ch: 银鬃铁卫从地下撤离了以后，下层区的住民们自发组织了维护秩序的民兵组织，代行银鬃铁卫的职责。
t_en: After the Silvermane Guards withdrew, and in order to keep the peace, the residents of the Underworld spontaneously organized their own militia — thereby taking over the Guards' duties.

t_ch: 我们要和「地火」联手吗？
t_en: Do we need to ally with Wildfire?

t_ch: 「联手」？此时聊这个为时尚早。
t_en: Ally? It's a little premature to be talking about that.

t_ch: 但「地火」熟悉地下大大小小的势力，如果我们想在地下找到星核的线索，他们会很有帮助。
t_en: But Wildfire will be familiar with all manner of forces below ground. If we want to find Stellaron clues in the underground, I'm certain they can help.

t_ch: 你信任「地火」吗？
t_en: Do you trust Wildfire?

t_ch: 没有了解，信任无从谈起。不过既然言及于此，我建议你不要太过于信任桑博•科斯基。
t_en: I don't know them. Trust isn't part of the picture yet. But speaking of trust, I suggest you don't place too much of yours in Sampo Koski.

t_ch: 此人从未展示出自己的意图，仅仅通过手段就将我们推到如今这个进退两难的局面，这样的人最是危险不过。
t_en: He hasn't revealed his true intentions, but he has managed to corral us into a difficult situation — almost without us realizing. That's a dangerous person indeed.

t_ch: 我已经清楚了。
t_en: Well, I'm all up to speed now.

t_ch: 嗯，这就是目前需要整理的情报了。如果还有什么不清楚的，随时过来找我。
t_en: Mm, that's all we need to summarize for now. If there's anything else you're not clear on, come and find me.

t_ch: 哟，你们就是奥列格介绍的贵客吧？欢迎入住歌德大饭店。
t_en: Oh, you must be Oleg's guests? Welcome to the Goethe Grand Hotel.
Goethe...?

t_ch: 嗯，你是不是见过地上那家气派的歌德宾馆呀？
t_en: Yep, you've probably seen that stylish Goethe Hotel above ground, right?

t_ch: 啊，有些不好的回忆涌上来了……
t_en: Uhh, not the most pleasant memories of that place...

t_ch: 那是咱们「大饭店」的分店。几百年前，歌德家一位前辈白手起家，一心相信自己能做大做强。
t_en: That's the above-ground branch of the Grand Hotel. A few hundred years ago, one ancestor of the Goethe family was ambitious, and sought to build something big from nothin'.

t_ch: 最后他终于完成了梦想，在地上最黄金的地段开了自己的分店。
t_en: He eventually realized his dream and opened a hotel branch in the most expensive area on the surface.

t_ch: 「大」饭店…？
t_en: Grand Hotel...?

t_ch: 哎，太失礼啦。
t_en: Hey, that's not very polite~

t_ch: 哈哈哈，没事，你也不是第一位有这反应的客人了。
t_en: Hahaha, it's fine. You aren't the first guest to have that reaction.

t_ch: 几百年前，歌德家一位前辈白手起家，一心相信自己能做大做强。后来他也的确做到了。
t_en: A few hundred years ago, one ancestor of the Goethe family was ambitious, and sought to build something big from nothin'. Eventually, he actually did it.

t_ch: 你们见过那家气派的歌德宾馆吗？严格来说，那其实是咱们「大饭店」的分店哦。
t_en: Have you seen that stylish Goethe Hotel? Strictly speaking, it's a branch of this Grand Hotel.

t_ch: …有梦想谁都了不起，好励志呀。
t_en: ...People with big dreams have big determination.

t_ch: 奥列格要我好好款待你们。钥匙拿好嘞，是咱们这儿最宽敞的客房。
t_en: Oleg wants me to take good care of y'all. Here's the key, you'll be stayin' in our most spacious guest room.

t_ch: 怎么说，{NICKNAME}？今天就这么歇了，还是再四处转转？
t_en: What do you think, {NICKNAME}? Should we go ahead and call it a day, or walk around a bit more first?

t_ch: 疲了，回房间休息吧。
t_en: I'm tired. Let's go back to the room to rest.

t_ch: 走，再去镇上逛逛。
t_en: Let's go explore the town a bit more.

t_ch: 再多逛一会儿吧。
t_en: Let's explore a bit more.

t_ch: 又是休息时间，希望这次别再出什么幺蛾子了。
t_en: Time to rest again. I hope there aren't any surprises this time.

t_ch: 那个奥列格，起码看上去还挺诚恳的——你们说呢？
t_en: That Oleg... he seems sincere. What do you guys think?

t_ch: 感觉是个好人。
t_en: I think he's a good person.

t_ch: 你怎么不来句「知人知面不知心」？
t_en: Don't you have something cool to say, like "Appearances can be deceiving"?

t_ch: 我又不是杠精。
t_en: I don't try to disagree.

t_ch: 知人知面不知心。
t_en: Appearances can be deceiving.

t_ch: 骄傲吧？{NICKNAME}说话已经有你的范儿了。
t_en: *sigh* Happy now? {NICKNAME} is even starting to sound like you.

t_ch: 与我无关。
t_en: It has nothing to do with me.

t_ch: 我不发表意见。
t_en: No comment.

t_ch: 你看{NICKNAME}，特别洒脱地说出完全不负责任的台词。
t_en: Would you look at {NICKNAME}, blurting out something so irresponsible.

t_ch: 倒是很像你。
t_en: A lot like you, actually.

t_ch: 不过问我的话，只能说「防人之心不可无」。
t_en: But if you ask for my opinion, it's better to be safe than sorry.

t_ch: 听你的，防着防着，毕竟上次在旅店被人捅刀子仿佛就在昨天。
t_en: Fine, we'll keep our guards up. Our last hotel experience was eventful, to say the least.

t_ch: 今晚派人守夜吧。
t_en: One of us should keep watch tonight.

t_ch: 守、守夜？这么复古吗？！那不是奇幻小说里的冒险团才会干的事吗？
t_en: K—Keep watch? Are we space cowboys now? I guess this is the cosmic wild west.

t_ch: 呵…呵欠，突然有股睡意……
t_en: Hehe... *pretend yawn* Anyone else sleepy...?

t_ch: 我看三月挺精神的。
t_en: March looks the most awake.

t_ch: 不、不行啦！我从来没通宵过哦？熬夜有多伤皮肤你知道吗！
t_en: N—No I don't! *pretend yawn* Besides, I've never pulled an all-nighter before! Do you know how bad it is for your skin?

t_ch: 以身作则吧——丹恒？
t_en: How about you set us a good example, Dan Heng?

t_ch: 我对睡眠的需求不高，我来吧。
t_en: Sure. I don't have much need for sleep.

t_ch: 守夜这事我当仁不让。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 你似乎很疲惫。不用勉强自己，交给我吧。
t_en: You seem pretty exhausted, don't overexert yourself. I'll do it.

t_ch: 我来守夜吧。
t_en: I'll keep watch.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅，你跟那个奥列格聊完啦？他没故意刁难你吧？
t_en: Bronya, did you finish talking to Oleg? Hope he didn't make things difficult for you.

t_ch: 没什么。话不投机，有些事我们没法达成共识。
t_en: Not really, though it wasn't the friendliest conversation. We didn't see eye to eye on everything.

t_ch: 一下子得知了太多，今晚我怕是睡不着了…要是你们信得过我，就把放哨的事就交给我吧。
t_en: I've come to know a great deal in a short time — I doubt I'll be able to fall asleep tonight.

t_ch: 不是信不过你哈，但让你守夜，我还不大放心…你一个铁卫军官跑到地下来，出事的可能性比我们还大吧。
t_en: I doubt I'll be able to sleep either... not that I don't trust you! It's just... you're a Silvermane Guard officer in the underground. There might be people looking to harm you.

t_ch: 如果他们有近似的想法，我也不能怪他们…银鬃铁卫确实欠下层人的。
t_en: I wouldn't be surprised if there are... the Silvermane Guards have a lot to make amends for down here.

t_ch: 如果当初能有人站出来，跟可可利亚大人讲清楚那样做的后果…算了，马后炮没有意义。
t_en: If someone had made a stand in the outset... and explained the consequences to Madam Cocolia... Forget it. It's meaningless to talk about it now.

t_ch: 不必担心我的安全，「地火」的奥列格保证不会伤害我。我相信他那样的人会说到做到。去休息吧。
t_en: You don't need to worry about me. Oleg guaranteed that I wouldn't be harmed. He seemed like a man of his word. Go and rest.

t_ch: 床摸上去不太柔软，但相比上层区宾馆室内冰冷的装潢，这间客房显然更有家的味道。
t_en: The bed isn't very soft, but this room feels more homey than the coldly-decorated Overworld hotel.

t_ch: 好困…希望这次能睡个好觉。
t_en: So tired... hope I can get a good night's sleep.

t_ch: （又是那个声音…这个梦也太诡异了。）
t_en: (That voice again... such a strange dream.)

t_ch: （毫无睡意，起来走走吧。）
t_en: (May as well go for a walk if I can't sleep.)

t_ch: 我失去她了…我的「愿望」，你们所谓的计划…这一切还有什么意义？
t_en: I lost her... My wish, your so-called plan... What is the point of it all?

t_ch: 舍弃颓丧…舍弃悲伤…舍弃无用的感情……
t_en: Your grief... sorrow... useless sentiments... abandon them all...

t_ch: 闭嘴！她是我的女儿，她是我唯一的……
t_en: Silence! She is my daughter, my only...

t_ch: 你们懂什么，没有灵魂的东西？你们永远不会明白…永远！
t_en: You soulless thing — how could you begin to fathom!? You will never understand... Never!

t_ch: 你没有失去她…她在另一头等着和你团聚。
t_en: You did not lose her... She waits to be reunited with you on the other side.

t_ch: 「新世界」……
t_en: A new world...

t_ch: 站起来…守护者……
t_en: Rise... guardian...

t_ch: 完成你的宿命…完成一个「母亲」的愿望。
t_en: Fulfill your destiny... Fulfill a mother's wish.

t_ch: 是谁！谁在那？
t_en: Who goes there? Show yourself!

t_ch: …原来是你。下次别再从背后接近了，你该庆幸我手上没拿武器。
t_en: ...It's you. Don't sneak up behind me next time. Lucky for you I wasn't armed.

t_ch: 你也睡不着吗？
t_en: No sleep for you either, huh?

t_ch: 最近总是噩梦缠身。
t_en: I've been having nightmares recently.

t_ch: 我懂你的感觉，烦心事太多，总把恐惧转化为梦境。
t_en: I understand. The more that weighs on your mind, the more your fears become dreamscapes.

t_ch: 有点焦虑未来。
t_en: I'm worried about the future.

t_ch: 那你我彼此彼此，我讨厌身不由己的感觉，但是现在……
t_en: I know what you mean. I don't like the feeling that I'm not in control. And now...

t_ch: 不工作8小时我睡不着。
t_en: I can't sleep unless I've worked for eight hours.

t_ch: …需要有个人管管你了。
t_en: ...You need someone to look after you.

t_ch: 正好，这里没有旁人…我能问你些事吗？
t_en: Actually, seeing as there's no one around... can I ask you some things?

t_ch: 那个所谓的「{RUBY_B#灾祸的源头}星核{RUBY_E#}」…假设真的找到了它，你们有多大把握能阻止寒潮？
t_en: This {RUBY_B#Source of the Disaster}Stellaron{RUBY_E#} you speak of... if you were to find it, how sure are you that you could stop the Eternal Freeze?

t_ch: 百分之百！
t_en: <unbreak>100%</unbreak>!

t_ch: 你表现得这么自信，我反而心虚了…这可不像帮小孩子捏雪球那么简单啊。
t_en: Your confidence actually makes me even more worried... This isn't like helping a child make a snowball.

t_ch: 我不知道。
t_en: I don't know.

t_ch: 这样吗…谢谢，你这么坦诚，我心里反而稍微踏实了些。
t_en: I see... Well, thank you for being sincere. In itself, it's a comfort to me.

t_ch: 这得问丹恒。
t_en: You'd better ask Dan Heng.

t_ch: 他是你们的领袖吗？感觉他不管做什么事，都很笃定。
t_en: Is he your leader? I feel he possesses a great stillness of mind in everything he does.

t_ch: 其实，我很难相信你们的说法，对于一直生活在贝洛伯格的人而言，你们提到的那些名词太遥远了……
t_en: You know, it was difficult for me to believe you. Some of the words you use are too alien for those that have lived their entire life in Belobog.

t_ch: 在我看来，可可利亚大人的命令——将你们逮捕才是合理的。但母亲一开始并没有这么做……
t_en: As far as I was concerned, Madam Cocolia's order to arrest you sounded reasonable... but why didn't she issue it straight away?

t_ch: 所以她一定在你们身上看到了什么…一些我尚且觉察不出的东西。这让我很困扰……
t_en: She must have realized something about you... Something I haven't yet been able to perceive for myself, and it bothers me...

t_ch: 我不知道到底发生了什么，她为什么突然转变了态度？…但命令就是命令。军人不该过度揣摩命令。
t_en: What must have transpired to cause her such a drastic change? ...Still, orders are orders. It's not for soldiers to question their superiors.

t_ch: 你也是身不由己。
t_en: You had no choice.

t_ch: 你不必替我开脱，我也不止一次怀疑过自己，但履行义务的责任心总是会占到上风。
t_en: I don't need you to absolve me. The truth is, I didn't have every confidence that I was doing the right thing, but the sense of duty to carry out an order is bound to prevail with me.

t_ch: 盲从约等于愚蠢。
t_en: Only a fool follows orders blindly.

t_ch: 这不用你提醒，我也不止一次怀疑过自己，但履行义务的责任心总是会占到上风。
t_en: I don't need you to remind me, and I didn't have every confidence that I was doing the right thing. But the sense of duty to carry out an order is bound to prevail with me.

t_ch: 过去，上层区的士兵们不断在前线丢掉性命，但裂界扩张的脚步却丝毫没有减缓。
t_en: In the past, Overworld soldiers on the front line would lose their lives in droves, and yet the Fragmentum's advance would remain unaffected.

t_ch: 可可利亚大人决定背水一战，她认为假如地面失守，少了地上供给的物资，地底立刻就会崩溃；但反之则能支撑更久。
t_en: Madam Cocolia decided on a last-ditch offensive. She thought that if the surface fell, the underground would immediately collapse without source of supply. However, in the reverse scenario, humanity would hold out longer.

t_ch: 于是她把所有银鬃铁卫调去前线，封锁上下层的往来，只保留供给和地髓交换的通道。这个决策，我曾经是认同的……
t_en: Nevertheless, that was her reason for deploying all the Silvermane Guards to the front line. The transport line for supplies and Geomarrow remained open, but the passage between the surface and the underground ceased. I approved of that decision...

t_ch: 可是缺少了银鬃铁卫的下层居然残败到这般地步…或许母亲的决定错了……
t_en: I never thought that the Underworld would sink into such ruin without the Silvermane Guards... Perhaps mother was wrong...

t_ch: 可我改变不了她的想法。我试过了，但她听不进去。我找不到方法……
t_en: But how can I change her mind? I've tried, but she won't listen. I don't know how to get through to her...

t_ch: 我们会帮你。
t_en: We'll help you.

t_ch: 帮我让守护者大人改变主意？她不是那么容易回心转意的人，这点没人比我清楚。
t_en: Help me change the mind of the Madam Guardian? Not easy. Nobody knows that better than I do.

t_ch: 你当「大守护者」就行了。
t_en: You should become the supreme guardian.

t_ch: 你的意思是……
t_en: You mean...

t_ch: 不行，这个念头太可怕了！绝对不行，不可能！
t_en: ...What was I thinking!? No... I shouldn't... That was a diabolical thought!

t_ch: 越想越乱，越乱越想，根本找不着方向…我只是希望贝洛伯格的人民能过上更好的生活……
t_en: The more I think about it, the more disoriented I get, which in turn makes me think about it even more... I just want a better life for the people of Belobog...

t_ch: 这世界比你想的要复杂。
t_en: The world is more complicated than you think.

t_ch: 我们互相帮助，或许有转机。
t_en: Let's work together, maybe we can make a change.

t_ch: 单枪匹马阻止不了末日降临。
t_en: You can't save the world alone.

t_ch: …或许你说的对，或许不对。现在我…还要想想。
t_en: ...Maybe you're right, or not. Right now I just... need more time to think.

t_ch: 陪我在这地下走走吧，暂时忘掉这些烦心事。
t_en: Walk with me. Let's forget our problems for a while.

t_ch: …我知道了，那我就去一趟铆钉镇，帮你把东西拿回来。
t_en: ...I know. I'll go to Rivet Town and bring them back for you.

t_ch: 你一个人去也太冒险了，让奥列格派人和你一起吧？
t_en: It's too dangerous to go alone. Get Oleg to send someone with you.

t_ch: 大叔他们都好几天没合眼了，还是先歇歇吧，硬撑着上，也帮不上我的忙啊，反而累赘。
t_en: The rest of Wildfire has barely gotten any sleep the last few days. They should rest. They'd only be holding their eyelids open and slowing me down.

t_ch: 再者，你不是说了吗？有些矿工伤势很重，时间就是生命啊……
t_en: Besides, didn't you say some of the miners were gravely injured? Lives are on the line...

t_ch: 遇到什么麻烦了吗？
t_en: Is there trouble?

t_ch: 我们来得真是时候。
t_en: Sounds like we arrived just on time.

t_ch: 你们不是在休息吗，这个点出门做什么？
t_en: Aren't you supposed to be sleeping? What are you doing out here?

t_ch: …没打什么坏主意吧？
t_en: ...Up to no good, perhaps?

t_ch: 睡不着觉出来走走而已，请你收回无端的指控。
t_en: We're just having a walk. Neither of us could sleep. You can rescind your baseless accusations.

t_ch: 哼，那去别处走吧——
t_en: Hmph. Walk somewhere else, then...

t_ch: 希儿，别急啊，他们也许能帮上你的忙。
t_en: Easy, Seele, maybe they can help.

t_ch: 我们愿意帮助娜塔莎小姐。
t_en: Always willing to help Miss Natasha.

t_ch: 哈哈，多谢你们啦。
t_en: Haha, I'm grateful.

t_ch: 脾气这么大，我才不帮呢。
t_en: We're not helping anyone with a temper like that.

t_ch: 别这样嘛。希儿是典型的嘴硬心软，她没有恶意的。
t_en: Hey, come on now, Seele's got a sharp tongue but a soft heart. She didn't mean anything.

t_ch: 没问题，我们什么忙都帮。
t_en: No problem. We'll help with anything.

t_ch: 是吗…怪不得你身上散发着主人翁的气质。
t_en: Haha, oh yeah? Well look at you swaggering in like the protagonist in your own story.

t_ch: 是这样的，之前矿区那件事很多人都受了伤，诊所人满为患了。你们帮忙平息了骚乱后，希儿和我一直忙着照顾伤员。
t_en: Here's the situation: The clinic is packed full of people injured in the mines incident. Since you helped us resolve things, Seele and I have been busy caring for them.

t_ch: 你也知道，地下的资源一直很紧缺，加上事发突然，诊所的医疗物资快要见底了，我们得从别处弄一些过来。
t_en: As you know, the underground is lacking resources to begin with. Add on the sudden nature of the incident, and it's no surprise that the clinic is almost out of medical supplies. We need to acquire more... somehow.

t_ch: 地下还有别的镇子吗？
t_en: Are there other towns in the underground?

t_ch: 嘿，别的镇子的状况比我们还差呢。
t_en: *sigh* The situation in other towns is even worse than ours.

t_ch: 把桑博给抓来？
t_en: Get Sampo to acquire some?

t_ch: 你还不了解他？只要是重要的事，你根本不能指望桑博这种人。
t_en: You don't know him at all yet, do you? You can't rely on people like Sampo when it's important.

t_ch: 矿区之所以流浪者越来越多，就是因为裂界在地下蔓延，到处都是游荡的怪物，每个镇子都深受其扰。
t_en: As the Fragmentum spreads underground, more monsters go roaming around causing trouble for all the towns. That means more vagrants in the mines.

t_ch: 赖以为生的物资设备都被裂界吞掉了，想从里边捞点东西出来，都得冒生命危险！
t_en: The towns' resources have been swallowed up by the Fragmentum. To get anything now, people have to risk their lives!

t_ch: …别这么激动，希儿，这对身体不好。
t_en: ...Easy now, Seele. Anger isn't good for one's health.

t_ch: 我和希儿其实都不是在这镇上长大的。我们来自北边的一座工业小镇，它的主城区几年前被裂界渗透了。
t_en: Seele and I actually aren't from here. We both come from an industrial town to the north. A few years ago, the Fragmentum began to seep into the town center.

t_ch: 我原本在那管理一家孤儿院兼诊所，但侵蚀发生了，就不得不搬来这磐岩镇。毕竟得先保证自己能活下去，才有机会救治更多的人。
t_en: I used to manage an orphanage and clinic there, but after the Fragmentum corroded the area, I had no choice but to move here to Boulder Town. After all, I have to survive myself if I want to save more people.

t_ch: 是要去被侵蚀的城区里收集可用的物资吗？算我一个。
t_en: Go to a corroded town to collect some useful resources? I'm in.

t_ch: 我们也来帮忙吧。
t_en: We'll help, too.

t_ch: 你想怎么做？听你的。
t_en: Alright, what's the plan?

t_ch: 不要明白得这么快啊！
t_en: How come you catch on this quick!?

t_ch: 你要一起来吗？我看你很疲惫的样子。
t_en: You want to come as well? But you look quite tired.

t_ch: 慢着！我还没答应让你加入呢！
t_en: Hold up! I never said you could join us!

t_ch: 事关人命，多一个帮手没坏处吧。正好我有不少应付裂界的经验，一定帮得上忙。
t_en: People's lives are at stake. Why wouldn't you want another person to help? Besides, I have some experience with the Fragmentums. I'm sure I will be of use.

t_ch: 哼，那……你别扯我的后腿！
t_en: *sigh* Well... just don't slow me down!

t_ch: ——希儿没有拒绝，就是已经认可你们啦。有你们陪着她，我也能放心了。
t_en: —Seele's lack of outright rejection means she approves of you, hehe. With you all accompanying her, my mind will be more at ease.

t_ch: 来吧，我给你们列一个物资清单。
t_en: Come. Let me give you a list of materials we need.

t_ch: 你们要去的地方叫铆钉镇，顺着北方的山路走下去，不用多久就能看到它了。
t_en: The place you are going to is called Rivet Town. Follow the path through the mountains to the north for a bit and you'll arrive there.

t_ch: 让我想想…用来固定断骨的钢板和绷带快用完了。铆钉镇的中心有一片集市，镇民撤离的时候还堆着不少货物。辛苦你们去找找吧。
t_en: Let me think... I'm almost out of metal plates and bandages used for immobilizing broken bones. There's a market in the center of Rivet Town. Residents left behind piles of materials there when they evacuated. Please look for some there...

t_ch: 然后是医用酒精——哈哈，我在孤儿院囤了不少，因为孩子们经常打架。撤离的时候我没有带走…希望还没过期。
t_en: Oh! Also, rubbing alcohol. I kept a bunch at the orphanage, since the kids often got into tussles. I didn't bring it with me when I left... I hope it's still intact.

t_ch: 最后是止痛剂。应该还有一批存在旧实验室里，希望没被怪物毁掉……
t_en: ...Lastly, painkillers. There should be a bunch stored in the old lab. Hopefully they haven't been destroyed by monsters...

t_ch: 有这些应该就够应付一阵子了，你们一路小心，千万不要勉强自己。
t_en: These should be enough to get by for a bit longer. Be careful. Don't push yourselves too hard.

t_ch: 钢板和绷带，医用酒精，还有止痛剂…记住了，小菜一碟。
t_en: Metal plates and bandages, rubbing alcohol, and painkillers... Got it, piece of cake.

t_ch: 出发吧，争取在傍晚之前赶回来。早点把那些病号安顿好，我就能早点从他们的鬼哭神嚎里解脱出来。
t_en: Let's go, if we leave now we can still make it back before sundown. When it comes to helping injured people groaning in pain, the sooner the better.

t_ch: 感谢你，{NICKNAME}。要不是遇到你们两个，希儿就要一个人去裂界冒险了。
t_en: Thank you, {NICKNAME}. If we hadn't run into you two, Seele would've had to venture into the Fragmentum on her own.

t_ch: 我知道她不是第一次应付这种事，但一想到她要对付那些畸形的怪物…我总是止不住会担心。
t_en: I know it's not her first rodeo, but the thought of her taking on those horrid monsters... I can't help but worry.

t_ch: 你好像格外关心她。
t_en: You seem to care about her a lot.

t_ch: 毕竟我一路看着她长大，可不希望她有个三长两短。
t_en: Well, she grew up before my eyes after all. I don't want anything bad to happen to her.

t_ch: 我觉得她能应付得来。
t_en: I think she can handle it.

t_ch: 我知道。但毕竟我是一路看着她长大的，可不希望她有个三长两短。
t_en: I know, but she grew up before my eyes after all. I don't want anything bad to happen to her.

t_ch: 希儿她从小就是这个性格，好强，冲动，对陌生人来说有些难以接近……
t_en: Seele has been like this since she was little — impulsive and eager to be the best. For people who don't know her, they might find her... difficult...

t_ch: 但她其实是值得托付性命的伙伴。只要再相处一段时间，你们会明白的。
t_en: But you can trust her, even with your life. Stick around a bit longer and you'll see what I mean.

t_ch: （不能跑太远，跟着布洛妮娅吧。）
t_en: (This is a little far, I should follow Bronya.)

t_ch: 不知多久没像这样散步了，想不到会在陌生的地方，跟刚认识不久的人一起。
t_en: I can't remember the last time I went for a walk like this. I never thought I'd take one through such a strange place, with someone I'd only just gotten to know.

t_ch: 这里好安静啊，我都有点不习惯了…周围越安静，脑海里的杂念就越吵闹。
t_en: It's so quiet here I'm not used to it... *sigh* The quieter things are around you, the louder they are in your head.

t_ch: 我想，大矿区里的流浪者也许是走投无路了吧？居然把手伸向无辜的矿队。
t_en: I'm surprised the vagrants in the Great Mine go after the mining teams, they must be desperate.

t_ch: 但抛开个中背景或苦衷，罪行就是罪行。唉，面对这么复杂的情况，执法者到底该如何在公义和情理之间找到平衡……
t_en: Still, whatever their situation, a crime is a crime. *sigh* How is the law supposed to find a balance between justice and compassion in the face of such complexity...

t_ch: 抱歉，我本想暂时抛下这些烦心事的…但果然还是逃不开。
t_en: I'm sorry, I said I'd forget such problems for a while... And, as expected, here they are keeping me company.

t_ch: 「地火」…我对他们几乎没有了解。在上层区的这么多年里，我从来没听说过这个组织。
t_en: Wildfire... I don't know anything about them. In all my time above ground, I never heard of this organization.

t_ch: 但他们的领袖还算通情达理。我和那位奥列格，虽然在认知上有些分歧，但至少尊重彼此的想法。
t_en: But their leader seems like a reasonable individual. Oleg and I have our differences in understanding, but we respect each other's opinion.

t_ch: …你靠手头上的物资还能撑多久？
t_en: ...How much longer can you last with your current supplies?

t_ch: 那是…希儿的声音？
t_en: Is that... Seele?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，我们上去看看。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, let's go take a look.

t_ch: 要是我们没有刚好路过…你真打算一个人冲进裂界？
t_en: If we hadn't happened to be passing by... were you really planning to go into the Fragmentum alone?

t_ch: 对啊，这有什么大惊小怪的，我早就习惯了。
t_en: Of course. What's all the fuss about? I've done that loads of times.

t_ch: 太鲁莽了…根据银鬃铁卫的军规，巡查裂界至少需要四人为一组，还必须配备专门的通讯官。
t_en: So reckless... According to the rules of the Silvermane Guards, any expedition into the Fragmentum must have a squad of at least four members, as well as a communications officer.

t_ch: 你也说了，那是银鬃铁卫的规矩——别把它套在我们身上。
t_en: Exactly, a rule of the Silvermane Guards. Don't apply your rules to us.

t_ch: 哈…下层的事总算是告一段落了，可以去找「星核」啦。
t_en: Ahhhh... Well, now that we're done with that whole situation in the Underworld, we can finally look for the Stellaron.

t_ch: 不过回想一下，感觉有点对不起克拉拉…我们本来答应她跟史瓦罗和平交谈来着……
t_en: *sigh* But thinking about it, I feel a bit bad about Clara... We promised her that our talks with Svarog would go peacefully...

t_ch: 是史瓦罗先动的手。
t_en: Svarog attacked first.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}已经尽力说服他了。没能事先准备得再周全些，是我的责任。
t_en: {NICKNAME}, you tried your best to persuade him. It's my fault for not preparing better for such a situation in advance.

t_ch: 抱歉，没能说服他…
t_en: Sorry, I wasn't able to persuade Svarog...

t_ch: 你尽力了，不用有负担，事先考虑不周是我的疏漏。况且，那时是史瓦罗先动手的，没别的办法。
t_en: You tried your best, don't blame yourself. I should've considered such a situation in advance. And it was Svarog who attacked first, we didn't have any other choice.

t_ch: 忘了加点「口才」。
t_en: I forgot to be more diplomatic.

t_ch: 我们根本没有这套系统。
t_en: That certainly isn't our area of strength.

t_ch: 我不是让你们抢着分锅啦！
t_en: I didn't mean for you guys to start beating yourselves up over it!

t_ch: 往好了想，现在的结果也很不错啊？所有人都知道「星核」是罪魁祸首了，大家都愿意助我们一臂之力。
t_en: Look on the bright side — things turned out alright, didn't they? Now everyone knows that the real problem here is the Stellaron, and they're willing to help us out.

t_ch: 任务进展顺利极了！
t_en: All in all, the mission is going super smoothly!

t_ch: 但还有很多事情没有弄清楚。比如……
t_en: But we still have a lot left to figure out. For instance...

t_ch: …「星核」到底在哪。
t_en: ...Where's the Stellaron?

t_ch: 正是，我们缺少「星核」的具体坐标和方位……
t_en: Exactly. We don't even have the Stellaron's coordinates and location.

t_ch: 可可利亚态度的突然转变也非常奇怪。
t_en: Cocolia's sudden change in attitude is also very curious.

t_ch: …可可利亚为什么要迫害我们。
t_en: ...Why does Cocolia have it in for us?

t_ch: 正是，她突然改变了态度，背后一定有更深层的原因。
t_en: Exactly. There must be a reason for this sudden change in her attitude.

t_ch: 而且，我们仍然没有掌握「星核」的具体方位和坐标。
t_en: And, we still don't have the coordinates and location of the Stellaron.

t_ch: …桑博究竟是什么人？！
t_en: ...Who is this Sampo character!?

t_ch: …这就无关紧要了吧。
t_en: ...That's not of foremost concern.

t_ch: 重要的是，我们仍然没有掌握「星核」的具体方位和坐标，也没有弄清楚可可利亚态度变化的原因。
t_en: What's important is that we still don't have the exact location and coordinates of the Stellaron. Nor have we figured out the reason behind Cocolia's sudden change in attitude.

t_ch: 现有的线索还没法完整地拼凑起来……
t_en: We still haven't put all of the pieces together...

t_ch: 其实我做了好几次梦…
t_en: Actually, I've been having some dreams...

t_ch: 梦？
t_en: Dreams?

t_ch: 啊，记得你之前也提过做了怪梦什么的……
t_en: Oh, I remember you mentioned something about strange dreams before...

t_ch: 将前几次在睡梦中听到的低语告诉丹恒和三月七……
t_en: Telling Dan Heng and March 7th about the voices you heard in your recent dreams...

t_ch: …三次梦境都是相同的场景，都包括可可利亚和另一个「声音」……
t_en: ...All three dreams were the same, with Cocolia and that other voice...

t_ch: 要说是巧合也太怪了。
t_en: It would be weird to call it a coincidence.

t_ch: 我在想，或许这些片段并非无序的空想，其中会不会有某种规律？…只有你一直反复做这样的梦，原因是……
t_en: I'm wondering if these dreams aren't just random, if there is some meaning behind them... Maybe you're having them because...

t_ch: 是…{F#她}{M#他}体内的星核？
t_en: Because of... the Stellaron inside {F#her}{M#him}?

t_ch: 我是这么猜想的，但无法证明。
t_en: That's my hunch, but I have no proof.

t_ch: 唉，那等于没说…所以咱们回地面以后该怎么行动呢？咱们说了半天，特别激动人心，可好像啥结论也没得出来……
t_en: Well then that's as good as nothing for now... So, what should we do when we get back above ground? After all this talk, we're still back at square one...

t_ch: 聚沙成塔。先好好休息一晚，明天找「地火」的人一起研究吧。
t_en: We solve a puzzle one piece at a time. Let's get some rest. We'll talk to Wildfire tomorrow and get to the bottom of this.

t_ch: 何况，还有一个核心人物没有交流过——她和可可利亚的联系，没准会是破局的关键。
t_en: Also, there's still one more key character we haven't talked to yet. Her connection to Cocolia may be the key to cracking this mystery.

t_ch: 你看那边，看见了吗？那儿以前是整个铆钉镇最乱的街区，也是我长大的地方。
t_en: Over there, see that? That used to be the worst street in Rivet Town... and it's also where I grew up.

t_ch: 我在那儿跟着大哥大姐们混，成天想的都是该去哪儿整下一顿。直到奥列格首领把我拎了出来，送到孤儿院。
t_en: My friends and I used to wander those streets thinking about where to find our next meal. That is, until Chief Oleg got me out and took me to the orphanage.

t_ch: 然后我就跟着娜塔莎学读书、写字，十岁开始跟着奥列格到各个矿区去巡查，有时还会跟当地的混混对峙……
t_en: There I learned to read and write from Natasha. At the age of <unbreak>10</unbreak>, I started to patrol the mines with Oleg, occasionally getting into fights with the local thugs...

t_ch: 真好啊。
t_en: ...That sounds nice.

t_ch: 真好？你在挖苦我吗？
t_en: Nice? You being sarcastic with me?

t_ch: 抱歉，下层区的生活很艰辛，我不该说得这么轻描淡写。
t_en: Oh! No, sorry. Life in the Underworld is difficult, I shouldn't be speaking about it so lightly.

t_ch: 干嘛这么郑重其事…你这点最让人受不了了。
t_en: *sigh* You're always so serious... It really gets on people's nerves sometimes, you know?

t_ch: 我的意思是，希儿——我有些羡慕你。
t_en: Right... Uh, what I meant was... I kind of envy you, Seele.

t_ch: 从记事起，我每天的生活都在读书、礼教、训练中循环。
t_en: For as long as I can remember, my days have been an endless cycle of studying, etiquette lessons, and training.

t_ch: 每天都有人在耳边提醒我：「牢记你的身份，布洛妮娅」，「这有违筑城者的训诫，布洛妮娅」，「淑女不该说出如此粗鄙之语，布洛妮娅」……
t_en: Every day all I hear is "Remember who you are, Bronya," "This is against the Architects' Admonishments, Bronya," "Ladies shouldn't use such foul language, Bronya"...

t_ch: 有些人会羡慕这样的生活，但是我却有些…不自在。一辈子都重复着同样的几件事，所有的目标都由别人为你设立…这种感觉，你大概没法想象吧？
t_en: ...Some may envy this kind of life, but I have felt... trapped. When every choice and every goal has already been made for you... You probably can't imagine how that feels.

t_ch: …确实想象不出来。不过我更关心，你到底说了啥「粗鄙之语」？
t_en: ...No, I can't... But more importantly, what kind of "foul language" were you using?

t_ch: 「我这就代表筑城者的意志，把枪戳进你的鼻孔」！
t_en: In the name of the Architects, I shall stick this spear into your nostril!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，就这？这跟隔着靴子搔痒似的，不带劲。看来在你回去之前，我得好好教你几句黑话。
t_en: Hahaha, that's it? That's nothing. Looks like I'll have to teach you some Underworld slang before you go back.

t_ch: 不了不了。
t_en: No, no, that won't be necessary.

t_ch: 起码比戳鼻孔带劲儿……
t_en: It'll be better than poking people's nostrils at least...

t_ch: 真没想到，我竟然会在这里跟未来的「守护者」谈心。我从小就没见过几个上层区的人。只听大人们讲的故事，我还以为你们都是群傲慢又冷漠的家伙。
t_en: I never thought that I'd be here having a heart-to-heart with the future guardian. As a kid, I didn't meet many people who lived in the Overworld. I only heard stories from the grown-ups and figured you were all just a bunch of cold snobs.

t_ch: 我听几个铁卫老兵说过，在隔绝令下达以前，上层和下层并没有什么分别。大家吃的是相同的食物，聊的是相同的话题，庆祝的也是相同的节日。
t_en: I've heard from some Silvermane veterans that before the orders were made to seal off the Underworld from the Overworld, there was no difference between the two places. Everybody ate the same food, chatted about the same topics, celebrated the same festivals...

t_ch: 到了我们这一代，虽然成长的环境有些不同…但说到生活中的喜怒哀乐、还有人与人之间的牵绊，这些珍贵的情感一定还是相通的。
t_en: Even though times are different now, things like the joys and sorrows of life, the ties between people... These precious things must certainly still connect us all.

t_ch: 如果能有一座桥梁把上下层再次连结起来…我们一定还能回到那个不分你我的年代，并肩抵御寒潮和裂界。
t_en: If there is a way to bridge the gap between the two worlds, we can definitely go back to the time when you and I were not divided. When we could stand side by side against the Eternal Freeze and the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 我不像你，总结不出这么多大道理。不过…如果这就是你想看到的未来，我愿意跟你一起造这座桥。
t_en: I'm not like you. I don't have that many grand plans for the future. But... if that's the future you want, I'm willing to build this bridge with you.

t_ch: 谢谢你，希儿。你的信任对我来说很重要。
t_en: Thank you, Seele. Your trust is very important to me.

t_ch: 话、话说回来，下一步你打算怎么办？从史瓦罗那儿听到的事，对你肯定影响不小吧？
t_en: Speaking of which, what are you going to do next? What Svarog revealed must've made quite an impact, huh...

t_ch: …嗯。我原本以为自己已经做好心理准备了，但还是……
t_en: ...Yes. I thought I was prepared for anything, but...

t_ch: 只要我还是守护者继承人，就迟早会接触到那些真相。但母亲为什么要对我隐瞒这些，又为什么要我追捕知晓「星核」本质的外来者…我怎么也想不通。
t_en: As long as I am the guardian's successor, those truths will come out sooner or later. But why does my mother hide it from me, and why does she want me to hunt down the outsiders who know about the nature of the Stellaron... It just... it doesn't make sense.

t_ch: 思前想后，我现在能做的事就只有一件——当面向她问个明白。
t_en: I've thought it over. There's only one thing I can do — go ask her directly.

t_ch: 你…等等，你不会真要去吧？你一个人去？不行，这主意也太……
t_en: You... Hold on, you're not really going, are you? Alone? You can't, this plan is...

t_ch: 我已经想清楚了，希儿。我是可可利亚大人的女儿，这一点无论如何都不会改变。
t_en: I've already thought it through, Seele. I am Madam Cocolia's daughter. That will never change.

t_ch: 不管作为女儿还是作为银鬃铁卫，我都必须尽到当面谏言的责任。哪怕……
t_en: Be it my duties as her heir, or a Silvermane Guard, I must face my problems head-on. Even if...

t_ch: 布洛妮娅……
t_en: Bronya...

t_ch: 这个给你，希儿。请帮我把它转交给那几位外来者。如果我没能和你们会合…他们会知道该怎么做。
t_en: This is for you, Seele. Please help me pass it on to the outsiders. If... If I'm unable to see you again... They'll know what to do.

t_ch: …好，我明白了。你清楚自己想干什么，我再怎么反对也是白费力气。你就给我记住一件事——要是你碰上了什么危险，我拼了命也会去救你的。
t_en: ...Okay, I understand. You've made up your mind, and there's nothing I can say that will change it. But remember this — if you run into trouble, I will come to save you, no matter what.

t_ch: 那我等着你。
t_en: Then I'll be waiting for you.

t_ch: 你小时候也在这里看过风景吗？
t_en: So... did you come to this spot when you were a child?

t_ch: 当然了，只是那时候还不懂得这景象有多珍贵。
t_en: Of course. I just didn't appreciate how nice it was at the time.

t_ch: …真好啊。
t_en: ...Very nice.

t_ch: 你终于醒啦！我们都在外面转悠半天了。
t_en: Took you long enough sleepy head. We've been waiting all day!

t_ch: 我发现咱们的作息对不上啊，你要么大半夜就爬起来，要么就睡得昏天黑地。不好不好，团队协作方面你还需要加强呀。
t_en: I've noticed that our sleep routines don't match up. You either keep getting up in the middle of the night or snoozing away until the day is almost out. Unacceptable. You gotta work on your teamwork!

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你昨晚又做梦了吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, did you have a dream again last night?

t_ch: 这次没有。
t_en: Not this time.

t_ch: 原来如此，怪不得睡这么久。
t_en: ...So that's why you slept for so long.

t_ch: 梦到虎克和帕姆在吵架。
t_en: I dreamed that Hook and Pom-Pom got into a squabble.

t_ch: …那也怪吵的，我打赌虎克吵不赢列车长。
t_en: ...Huh, that's weird. I bet the conductor won, right?

t_ch: 不记得做没做梦了。
t_en: I can't remember.

t_ch: 啊，我也经常这样，好像做了梦，又记不得什么内容。
t_en: Oh, that happens to me all the time. It feels like I had a dream, but I can't remember anything about it.

t_ch: 那就去找「地火」商量后续的计划吧，看看他们有没有什么新发现。
t_en: Well then, let's go find Wildfire to discuss our next move and see if they have any new discoveries.

t_ch: 走吧！终于能回到上面去了，我都要等不及啦。
t_en: Let's go! I can't wait to finally get back above ground!

t_ch: 唷，看看是谁来了！
t_en: Oh! Look who's here!

t_ch: 是史瓦罗克星！下层区的大英雄——{NICKNAME}！
t_en: It's the bane of Svarog! The big hero of the Underworld — {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: ——大英雄…丹恒和三月七！
t_en: And the other big heroes, Dan Heng and March 7th!

t_ch: 反应挺快哈。
t_en: Were those lines rehearsed?

t_ch: 娜塔莎呢？她不在吗？
t_en: Where's Natasha? Is she here?

t_ch: 她还有很多事要善后，你们就跟我这个老头子聊吧，我说的话就代表她的意思。
t_en: She has a bunch of other things to attend to, so I hope you don't mind talking to this old man instead. I speak on behalf of Natasha.

t_ch: 说起来还真是对不住各位，我只是娜塔莎的代理这回事一直瞒着你们。哈哈哈。
t_en: By the way, sorry for keeping that whole thing about her being the actual leader of Wildfire a secret. Hahaha...

t_ch: 倒是没什么关系…
t_en: It doesn't matter anyway...

t_ch: 你们不计较就好。娜塔莎一向办事谨慎，但她绝对没有什么坏心思，这点想必你们也发现了。
t_en: I'm glad you don't mind. Natasha is always cautious, but she has no ill intent, as you surely have noticed.

t_ch: 人与人之间的信任呢？
t_en: What ever happened to trust?

t_ch: 哈哈哈，抱歉抱歉。娜塔莎做事一向谨慎，但她没什么坏心思，这点想必你们也发现了。
t_en: Hahaha, my apologies. Natasha is always cautious, but she has no ill intent, as you must have noticed.

t_ch: 没看出对不起的意思！
t_en: I'm not seeing the apology here!

t_ch: 没办法嘛，那时也不会知道你们会成为地下的大英雄，我作为明面上的首领，总不能一见面就出卖娜塔莎吧。
t_en: Oh, well. I might just be second in command, but I still have a lot of responsibility. I couldn't give Natasha up right when we first met, could I? I had no clue that you bunch would become big heroes of the Underworld.

t_ch: 她嘱咐我一定要保证你们安全回到上层。我思前顾后，最稳妥的办法还是拜托这小子帮忙。
t_en: She told me to make sure you return to the Overworld safely. I gave it some thought, and I think the safest way is to ask this fella for help.

t_ch: 我带你们下来，当然也得负责带回去～这是免费的售后服务，包君满意。
t_en: I brought you down. I can take you back up! Free of charge, satisfaction guaranteed.

t_ch: …这次用不着弄晕咱们了吧。
t_en: ...There's no need to knock us out this time, right?

t_ch: 不用不用，这次我们通过「炉心」上去。
t_en: Of course not! This time we'll go back through the Furnace Core.

t_ch: 那要你何用？
t_en: And why do we need you?

t_ch: 我…能给你们解个闷吧——玩笑！我开玩笑！这条路被封锁十几年了，没几个人知道怎么走，你们还是需要我的！
t_en: Well, for my sparkling companionship of course! ...I jest, I jest! The path has been blocked for over ten years, and very few know how to get through. That's where I come in.

t_ch: 你的通道贡献出来！
t_en: Just tell us the path you used before!

t_ch: 呃…那个很麻烦的，又麻烦又危险，我说真的，不如走「炉心」来得方便。
t_en: That would be... problematic, and dangerous. Trust me, we should go through the Furnace Core.

t_ch: 奥列格，给我换人！
t_en: Oleg, give us someone else!

t_ch: 别啊——咱们这么久的交情了，怎么还不相信我啊！
t_en: Please, don't be like that. We have so much history together, can't you have a little faith?

t_ch: 别扯没用的，当好你的向导！
t_en: Enough chatter. Just be a good guide!

t_ch: 等等，布洛妮娅呢？
t_en: Hold on. What about Bronya?

t_ch: …她为什么没来。
t_en: Why isn't she here?

t_ch: 布洛妮娅先回去了。她跟那位「大守护者」的关系你们也知道，有些事情她必须自己解决。
t_en: She already went back. As you know, she has some things to settle with the Supreme Guardian.

t_ch: 什么？她丢下我们，自己就这么回去了？你怎么不拦着她呀……
t_en: What? She just ditched us and went back? How could you let her...

t_ch: 她不打算帮我们了？
t_en: She's not going to help us?

t_ch: 才不是！她只是…有很多责任。虽然不大能理解，但我相信她在尝试用自己的方式解决问题。
t_en: It's not like that! She... just has many responsibilities to bear. I don't completely understand, I trust that she's trying to solve the problem.

t_ch: 她也有自己的打算。
t_en: She also has her own plan.

t_ch: 是的，她肩负了很多责任。虽然不大能理解，但我相信她在尝试用自己的方式解决问题。
t_en: Exactly, she has a lot of responsibilities. I don't completely understand, but I trust that she's trying to solve the problem.

t_ch: 她没带桑博不也走了？
t_en: She left without Sampo?

t_ch: 没完了是吧！我们走这趟路用得着桑博，等上去了再赶走他也不迟！
t_en: Enough about Sampo! We need him for this trip, and we can still get rid of him after we reach the surface.

t_ch: 希儿小姐，我还在这儿呢……
t_en: Miss Seele, I'm still here, you know...

t_ch: 对了，布洛妮娅让我把这个交给你们。
t_en: Oh right, Bronya told me to give you this.

t_ch: 她给我们留了封信，唔…难道是传闻中的「锦囊妙计」？我没经验，这个是不是应该遇到危险了再打开？
t_en: She left us a letter. Hmm... could this be one of those "open-in-case-of-emergency" letters? I've never gotten one of those before. Should we wait until we run into something dangerous?

t_ch: 别瞎想了，快拆开看看。
t_en: Stop overthinking and just open it.

t_ch: 朗道姐弟…？朗道弟我知道，姐是谁……
t_en: Brother and sister Landau...? I know the brother must refer to Gepard, but who could the sister be...

t_ch: 希露瓦…啊，原来她是杰帕德的姐姐！
t_en: Oh, Serval! So she's Gepard's older sister!

t_ch: 你的关注点好奇怪啊。
t_en: How very astute of you.

t_ch: 金发大美人和金发大帅哥，啧啧，遗传基因真厉害呀。
t_en: Blonde, tall, good-looking... *sigh* Those Landau genes sure are something else.

t_ch: 希望布洛妮娅别出意外。
t_en: I hope nothing happens to Bronya.

t_ch: 她已经考虑得很周全了。不过，那位大守护者的想法…即使是布洛妮娅怕也难以掌握。
t_en: She gave it some good thought, but whatever's going on in that supreme guardian's head... Even Bronya might have trouble understanding.

t_ch: 也许姐姐是指杰帕德呢。
t_en: Maybe the "sister" refers to Gepard.

t_ch: 你的关注点好奇怪啊！
t_en: Hey, stay on track!

t_ch: 桑博，你了解朗道姐弟吗？
t_en: Sampo, do you know the Landau siblings?

t_ch: 朗道，老朋友了，主要是和弟弟打交道多，姐姐嘛…比弟弟还可怕。今天是大喜的日子，就别聊这个了吧。
t_en: Landau... Uhh yeah, we're old friends. I've mostly dealt with the younger brother in the past, but the sister... *shudder* She's much scarier. Today's supposed to be a day of celebration, so let's not talk about it right now.

t_ch: 想出发了就来找我，咱们随时可以动身～
t_en: We can set out at any time. Just come find me when you're ready~

t_ch: 嘿，朋友们，咱们走呗？
t_en: Well my friends, are you ready to head out?

t_ch: 出发吧。
t_en: Let's go.

t_ch: 好嘞！吃的喝的可都得带足了，这一路可得爬不少阶梯啊。
t_en: Wonderful! Remember to bring enough food and drink to refuel on the way. We have a lot of steps to climb!

t_ch: 别急，问你点事。
t_en: Hold on, I have a question.

t_ch: 行，你问我答，知无不言。
t_en: Ask away, my friend. I have all the answers you seek.

t_ch: 说说杰帕德•朗道。
t_en: Tell me about Gepard Landau.

t_ch: 还说什么呢，也是你们的老朋友了。挺好的小伙子，正直刚毅，年轻帅气，在缺乏魅力这方面极具魅力。
t_en: What else is there to say? You know him as well as I do. He's an upstanding guy, as well as young, handsome, and charming in his lack of charm.

t_ch: 说实话，我和他交情不深，毕竟他是官兵我是贼。
t_en: But to be honest, I'm not very close to him. After all, he's an officer and I'm... the opposite.

t_ch: 说说希露瓦•朗道。
t_en: Tell me about Serval Landau.

t_ch: 呵，她可了不起，著名的朗道家族叛逆女，本来前途一片光明，结果得罪了领导，工作丢了，跑去开了间机械屋玩地下音乐。
t_en: Oh, she's quite extraordinary. The renowned rebellious Landau girl. She once had a bright future, but after getting on her leader's bad side, she lost her job. She ended up opening a workshop and playing underground music.

t_ch: 听说可把老爷子气得够呛，家里也就几个弟弟妹妹还在跟她来往。
t_en: I heard that her old man was furious, and that only a few younger siblings are still in contact with her.

t_ch: 在地上要是碰到机械相关的问题，我有时会去找她，后来嘛…因为一些特殊原因，就不怎么去光顾了。
t_en: I used to go to her sometimes when I had any mechanical problems above ground. But for... various reasons, I don't visit her workshop much anymore.

t_ch: 说说「炉心」。
t_en: Tell me about the Furnace Core.

t_ch: 连接地上下的通道，里面全是螺旋上升的石阶。我反正是搞不懂，拥有这么发达的科技，筑城者还执着于台阶是图什么，盖个电梯不好么？
t_en: It's a passage connecting the Underworld and the Overworld, a big, stone, spiral stairway. Y'know, I don't understand the Architects' obsession with making these steps when there is such advanced technology available. Wouldn't an elevator have been better?

t_ch: 你们年纪轻还好点，对我这种老年人来说，爬趟炉心膝盖都要废喽。
t_en: ...It's easier for you youngsters. For this old guy, the steps of the Furnace Core are a nightmare on the knees!

t_ch: 说说你自己。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: 问几遍了，还没听够吗？好吧，我叫桑博•科斯基，职业是行商，爱好是见义勇为，对你们没有任何企图。
t_en: You haven't heard enough about me, huh? Alright, my name is Sampo Koski. I'm a businessman who stands up for justice in his free time. And no, I don't have it in for you.

t_ch: 我问完了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 还有事没办完。
t_en: There's still unfinished business here.

t_ch: 怎么，舍不得地底啊？去吧去吧，我在这儿等着你们。
t_en: What's the matter? Got too attached to the underground? Go ahead, I'll be waiting here.

t_ch: 母亲大人，我回来了。
t_en: Mother, I have returned.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅！我还以为…我还以为要失去你了。
t_en: Bronya! I thought... I thought I'd lost you.

t_ch: 你到底跑哪去了，有没有受伤？我马上找管家过来——
t_en: Where were you? A—Are you hurt? I'll fetch the butler right away—

t_ch: 不，不用了！我现在不太想见塞巴斯。抱歉，母亲……
t_en: No, there's no need! I don't want to see Sebas right now. Sorry, mother...

t_ch: 我很好，真的很好。我在下层区跑了一大圈，经历了几场战斗，但总体还算应付得来。
t_en: I'm fine, really. I just got into a few skirmishes down in the Underworld, but nothing I couldn't handle.

t_ch: 下层区？啊…我知道了。
t_en: The Underworld? Oh... I see.

t_ch: 那就向我汇报一下你的见闻吧，布洛妮娅统领。
t_en: In that case, report everything you saw, Commander Bronya.

t_ch: 边缘通路的追捕失败以后，我和受通缉的「外来者」以某种方式被带到了下层区。
t_en: After the failed pursuit at Backwater Pass, the wanted outsiders and I were somehow taken to the Underworld.

t_ch: 我们迫于环境压力结成暂时同盟，一起帮下层区的居民解决了些…生存难题。
t_en: Because of the difficult situation we were in, we formed a temporary alliance to help the residents of the Underworld resolve a few... issues of survival.

t_ch: 我们还打败了名为史瓦罗的远古机器，了解到了一些真相…有关「星核」的真相。
t_en: We also defeated an ancient robot known as "Svarog," and learned some truths... about the Stellaron.

t_ch: 我在听…说下去。
t_en: ...I'm listening. Go on.

t_ch: 可可利亚大人，我以人格担保，那些「天外来客」绝非我们此前所设想的恶党。
t_en: Madam Cocolia, it is my firm belief that the visitors from beyond the sky are not the villains we had imagined before.

t_ch: 我看到他们为了公义拔枪，为了他人的理想拼上性命。我可以确认，他们的确是为了「星核」而来，为了解除它给贝洛伯格带来的灾难。
t_en: I witnessed them take up arms in the name of justice, and risk their lives for the sake of others. I can also ascertain that they did come for the Stellaron, but only to relieve the disaster it has brought upon Belobog.

t_ch: 母亲大人…您一直都清楚「星核」的真相，对吗？我总有一天也会接触到那些知识。成为「守护者」所需要付出的代价之一，就是永远背负这些秘密。
t_en: Mother... you've always known the truth about the Stellaron, haven't you? The responsibility of bearing that truth will fall upon me one day. Such is the burden of being guardian — to carry these secrets for eternity.

t_ch: 所以…原谅我的冒犯，我认为下令剿杀外来者是错误的选择。为了解决「星核」带来的问题，筑城者已经等了几百年，而他们几个可能就是——
t_en: So... I ask you to forgive my defiance, but I believe that the order to dispatch the outsiders was a mistake. To solve the problems brought on by the Stellaron, the Architects waited hundreds of years, and those outsiders may be the... the—

t_ch: 够了！
t_en: Enough!

t_ch: 傲慢，无知…你令我失望，布洛妮娅。
t_en: Arrogant, ignorant... Oh you disappoint me, Bronya.

t_ch: 你不过窥到了雪山的一角，就以为自己看清了真相。只是在下层溅了一身泥渣，居然就敢质疑我的命令？
t_en: You merely glimpsed the tip of the iceberg and now you think you know everything. You spend a short time with that Underworld scum, and suddenly you have the audacity to question my orders?

t_ch: 铁卫！把布洛妮娅——
t_en: Guards! Take Bronya—

t_ch: ——他们不是泥渣！
t_en: —They are not scum!

t_ch: 我的身世，那些你从来没告诉过我的事…我现在都已经记起来了。我不会再逃避了，守护者大人！请别再用含糊其辞的理由搪塞我了。
t_en: My entire life, everything you ever kept from me... I remember it clearly now. This time... this time I'm standing my ground, Madam Guardian! And please, stop dismissing me with vague excuses.

t_ch: 把你看到的东西告诉我吧，你到底在隐藏些什么…为什么要把铁卫派到裂界里去送死，为什么要放弃下层区的人民——为什么要在那个关头转变心意？
t_en: Tell me what you have seen. What exactly it is you are hiding... Why do you send the Silvermane Guards to die in the Fragmentum? Why have you abandoned the people of the Underworld? And why... why did you have that sudden change of mind?

t_ch: …我知道了。
t_en: ...I see.

t_ch: 这个时刻迟早会来…我只是没想到会来得这么快。
t_en: I knew this moment would come sooner or later... I just didn't expect it would be so soon.

t_ch: 你想知道是什么原因驱使我下达了那些命令…对吗？你觉得自己已经准备好触碰真相了？
t_en: You want to know why I gave those orders... is that right? Do you believe you are ready to learn the truth?

t_ch: 嗯。我准备好了…母亲。
t_en: Yes. I am ready... Mother.

t_ch: 那就跟我来吧，布洛妮娅。
t_en: Then come with me, Bronya.

t_ch: 这个濒死的世界…该让你听听她的声音了。
t_en: It is time you heard it... the voice of this dying world.

t_ch: 来吧…来吧……
t_en: Come... Come...

t_ch: 一股倦意涌上心头…提醒你已经很久没有休息过了。你坚信，这一次无论多么糟糕的梦境都不可能把你唤醒。
t_en: A wave of tiredness comes over you... reminding you that you haven't rested in a long time. Even the most terrible dream won't wake you up this time.

t_ch: 是该好好睡一觉了…
t_en: I suppose it would be good to get some sleep...

t_ch: 其实我还挺有精神的。
t_en: Actually, I still have a lot of energy.

t_ch: （啊…睡得真好。）
t_en: (Ahh, that was a good sleep.)

t_ch: （去跟三月和丹恒会合吧。）
t_en: (I should go meet up with March and Dan Heng.)

t_ch: 你们回来啦？听「地火」说，你们帮忙解决了史瓦罗那边的麻烦，可真了不起！
t_en: You're back! I've heard from Wildfire that you helped resolve that whole situation with Svarog. Amazing!

t_ch: 经历了这么多事，是不是累坏了？快吃点东西，然后好好休息一晚吧。
t_en: It must have been exhausting. Please, have some food and get a good night's rest.

t_ch: 我们想休息一下。
t_en: We would like to have a rest.

t_ch: 啊，鼹鼠党的荣誉队员！听我说！
t_en: Ah, honorary member of The Moles! Listen up!

t_ch: 怎么了，漆黑的虎克大人？
t_en: What is it, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great?

t_ch: 有什么事吗？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 没礼貌！你应该叫我漆黑的…算了算了，不说这个了！
t_en: How rude! You should have called me Pitch-Dark... Ugh, never mind...

t_ch: 上次找探测器的事，多谢你啦！现在虎克还有另外一件事想找你帮忙！
t_en: Thank you for helping Hook find the detector, but now Hook needs to ask you another favor...

t_ch: 包在我身上了。
t_en: Leave it to me.

t_ch: 虎克还没说是什么事呢！不过你干劲很足嘛，不愧是我看中的荣誉队员！
t_en: Haha, I haven't even mentioned what this is about! But that's the spirit. You truly are an honorary member of The Moles!

t_ch: 你先说来听听。
t_en: I'm all ears.

t_ch: 虎克和老爹的生日就快到了！每年这个时候，虎克和老爹会为对方准备好礼物，一起庆祝生日。
t_en: Hook and daddy's birthday is approaching! Every year at this time, we exchange gifts and celebrate our birthday together.

t_ch: 你们生日是同一天？这么巧？
t_en: You two share a birthday? What a coincidence!

t_ch: 对啊，有什么问题吗？
t_en: Yeah, what's the problem?

t_ch: 自从来到磐岩镇以后，老爹的工作就更辛苦了，每天都要很晚才回家。所以，虎克不想再用老爹给的零花钱买礼物了！
t_en: Daddy's job has been more difficult since moving to Boulder Town. He gets home really late every day. I don't want to use his hard-earned money to buy a gift!

t_ch: 虎克懂事了呀。
t_en: Hook, you are so considerate.

t_ch: 诶嘿嘿…
t_en: Hee-hee...

t_ch: 找娜塔莎要零花钱怎么样？
t_en: Why not ask Natasha for some money?

t_ch: 虎克打碎了很多诊所的东西，有点不好意思向老巫婆开口……
t_en: ...Oh, I've broken a thing or two in the clinic, you see... I'm too embarrassed to ask for help from the old witch...

t_ch: 其实，虎克还有一些值钱的宝贝落在铆钉镇里了。它们被丢弃在那里也很可惜，要是带回来再卖个好价钱，那么虎克给老爹买礼物就不用求任何人了。
t_en: Actually, I still have some valuable treasure of my own in Rivet Town. It's a shame I left it there. If I go bring it back and sell it for a decent price, I can buy a gift for daddy without begging anyone for money.

t_ch: 铆钉镇可是在裂界里…
t_en: Rivet Town is in Fragmentum...

t_ch: 虎克知道，铆钉镇现在很危险！所以我想请你当虎克的保镖！
t_en: I know that Rivet Town is super dangerous right now! That's why I'd like to enlist you as my bodyguard!

t_ch: 这样就算事后被老巫婆发现，她也不会只批评虎克一个人了——嘿嘿！
t_en: That way, even if the old witch finds out later, I won't be the only one getting a scolding, hehe!

t_ch: 你这不是在求我吗？
t_en: So you're begging me to help?

t_ch: 什、什么话！这是来自鼹鼠党老大的命令，对，命令！
t_en: Wh—What nonsense! This is an order from the boss of The Moles. An order!

t_ch: 而且虎克还是个小孩子！你不会想让我一个人进到裂界里去吧！
t_en: And I'm still a kid! You wouldn't let me go inside the Fragmentum by myself, would you?

t_ch: 那就这么约好了，我们在铆钉镇里见！虎克会到安全的地方等你的，可不许不来哦！
t_en: Then it's settled, let's meet in Rivet Town! I'll be waiting for you in a safe spot. You're not allowed to not come!

t_ch: 虎克有些值钱的宝贝落在铆钉镇里了……
t_en: I've got to get back to my treasure!

t_ch: 既然约好了，我们在铆钉镇里见！虎克会到安全的地方等你的，可不许不来哦！
t_en: Let's agree to meet in Rivet Town! I'll be waiting for you in a safe spot. You're not allowed to not come!

t_ch: 你这高高在上的语气是怎么回事呀！
t_en: Hey, are you patronizing me!?

t_ch: 请允许我拒绝！
t_en: Allow me to refuse.

t_ch: 你怎么比虎克还没有礼貌，至少听完之后再拒绝嘛！
t_en: Hmph, at least hear me out first before you refuse.

t_ch: 虎克？这是……
t_en: Hook? What are you...

t_ch: 娜、娜塔莎姐姐！「菌菌」坏掉了，我想请你帮忙修好……
t_en: N—Natasha! Junjun's in bad shape. Can you help me fix him...?

t_ch: 我记得它，这好像是我以前给你的？你是从哪把它找回来的？
t_en: I remember giving you Junjun. I haven't seen this fella in years. Where did you find him?

t_ch: 刮、刮大风，它被大风吹回来了……
t_en: The... the wind brought him back to me...

t_ch: 说实话，虎克。
t_en: Be honest with me, Hook.

t_ch: …是{NICKNAME}帮我一起，从铆钉镇的孤儿院找出来的。
t_en: ...{NICKNAME} helped me find him in the Rivet Town orphanage.

t_ch: 就为了这个玩偶，你就让{F#大姐姐}{M#大哥哥}带着你到裂界里去冒险？虎克，我跟你说过多少次……
t_en: You had {NICKNAME} take you to the Fragmentum just for this teddy bear? Hook, how many times have I told you...?

t_ch: ——求求你了，娜塔莎姐姐！请你帮虎克修好「菌菌」吧，虎克愿意帮你打扫一个月的诊所！
t_en: Natasha, please! Please help me fix Junjun. I'll clean the clinic for you for a whole month!

t_ch: 你每次都这么说，但是……
t_en: *sigh* You say that every time, and then...

t_ch: 我、我先跑了！{NICKNAME}，「菌菌」就交给你了！
t_en: G—Great, seeya soon! {NICKNAME}, I'll leave Junjun with you!

t_ch: 这孩子…真拿她没办法。
t_en: ...What am I going to do with that girl.

t_ch: 你也不要太溺爱她了，外来者。我知道想拒绝虎克是一件难事，但人还是得学会说「不」才行呢。
t_en: You shouldn't indulge her, outsider. Uh-uh, I know it's difficult to tell Hook "no," but sometimes it just has to be done.

t_ch: 「菌菌」…原来她还给这玩具熊起了名字吗？呵……
t_en: Junjun... so she gave her bear a name, huh?

t_ch: 我感觉这玩偶好像平平无奇。
t_en: It seems like a regular teddy bear to me.

t_ch: 没什么特别的，只是我做过的诸多玩偶之一。不过，虎克真的很喜欢它。
t_en: Yeah, I've lost count of all the dolls I've made. Still, Hook clearly likes this one a lot.

t_ch: 虎克好像挺喜欢这个玩偶的？
t_en: Hook seems really fond of this bear.

t_ch: 这不过是我做过的诸多玩偶之一。说实话，我也没想到她会这么喜欢它。
t_en: I've lost count of all the dolls I've made. To be honest, I wasn't expecting her to like this one so much.

t_ch: 还在铆钉镇的时候，这是我奖励给「好孩子」的奖品。孤儿院难管教的孩子实在太多，你能想象那场面有多混乱吗？我总得想点办法让那些熊孩子乖乖听话。
t_en: It was an award I used to give the kids in Rivet Town for "good behavior." Can you imagine what an orphanage is like with so many difficult children? I had to come up with a solution to get them to behave.

t_ch: 为了得到「菌菌」，虎克努力装了一个月的「乖宝宝」——按时吃饭，按时睡觉，也没有跟别的小朋友打架……
t_en: Hook pretended to be well-behaved for a month in order to get Junjun. She ate on time, slept on time — even stopped fighting with the other kids...

t_ch: 就算是演的，也很辛苦吧？
t_en: That must have been some difficult pretending...

t_ch: 确实，等她拿到奖品以后，那家伙立马就回归本性了。
t_en: Of course, as soon as she got her award, she went straight back to her old self.

t_ch: 说不定平时的样子才是装的…
t_en: Maybe she's actually a well-behaved kid pretending to be naughty...

t_ch: 你怕是不知道她那个月过得有多痛苦。
t_en: I'm afraid you have no idea how much she suffered that month.

t_ch: 虎克那孩子，总能把人们团结到自己身边，不管对方是大人还是小孩。这是一种天分——强迫她当个「乖孩子」，就是在扼杀这种天分。
t_en: Hook has an uncanny ability to rally people to her cause — both adults and children. It's a skill alright, and forcing her to be well-behaved puts a lid on it.

t_ch: …嗯，还真是一团糟呢。别着急，我会帮虎克修好「菌菌」的。也许我需要重新找些填充物……
t_en: ...Well, this teddy bear has sure been through the wars. Don't worry, I'll help Hook repair Junjun, but I'll need to find some stuffing...

t_ch: 等等，这玩偶熊的眼睛……
t_en: W—Wait, look at the eyes...

t_ch: 是块很漂亮的石头。
t_en: Pretty, they look like gemstones.

t_ch: 圆圆的，黑黑的。
t_en: Round and black.

t_ch: 这是…黑宝石？奇怪，当时我把它缝进玩具熊的时候，难道没发现吗……
t_en: They must be... erebus? Strange, I didn't notice when I sewed them on...

t_ch: 很值钱吗？
t_en: Are they worth a lot?

t_ch: 真是意外的黑宝石。
t_en: What a surprise...

t_ch: 我不是鉴定专家，但这两颗石头的成色…看上去相当不错呢。
t_en: I'm no appraiser, but these two gems look to be high quality.

t_ch: 我当时是得有多不小心，才会把这么值钱的石头缝到玩具熊身上？真是……
t_en: I think I must have been — it's not every day you sew precious gems into a teddy bear. Honestly...

t_ch: …老巫婆有没有批评你？
t_en: ...Did the old witch yell at you?

t_ch: 批评了。
t_en: She did.

t_ch: 唔，你明明是为了帮虎克…对不起，荣誉队员！
t_en: *sigh* But you were only there to help Hook... I'm sorry, honorary member!

t_ch: 没有批评。
t_en: She didn't.

t_ch: 什么？凭什么只有虎克挨批评了呀！
t_en: What? Why did she only yell at Hook!?

t_ch: 先不说这个了，你没有把虎克真正的目的说出去吧？
t_en: Never mind that. You didn't tell her what I was really up to, did you?

t_ch: 绝对没说，虎克大人。
t_en: Absolutely not, Pitch-Dark Hook the Great.

t_ch: 很好，不愧是忠诚的鼹鼠党荣誉队员！
t_en: Great! Your loyalty as an honorary member of The Moles is acknowledged.

t_ch: 娜塔莎的直觉可是很敏锐的。
t_en: Natasha has excellent intuition.

t_ch: …虎克知道！我只是…不想惹她生气！
t_en: ...I'm aware of that! I just... didn't want to upset her!

t_ch: 怎么了，你怎么还傻站在这儿，有什么要向鼹鼠党的老大报告吗？
t_en: Anyway, why are you still standing here, silly? Do you have something to report to the boss of The Moles?

t_ch: 其实「菌菌」的眼睛是宝石。
t_en: Junjun's eyes are precious gems.

t_ch: 宝、宝石！是很值钱的意思吗？
t_en: G—Gems!? Does that mean he's worth a lot of money?

t_ch: 要把它卖掉吗？
t_en: Will you sell him?

t_ch: 卖掉，当然要卖掉！如果宝石能卖个好价钱，那虎克就能给老爹准备更豪华的生日礼物啦！
t_en: Of course I'll sell him! If the gems are expensive, Hook can buy a better birthday gift for daddy!

t_ch: 突然不想卖了？
t_en: You don't want to sell him all of a sudden?

t_ch: 你在说什么呢，当然要卖掉！如果宝石能卖个好价钱，那虎克就能给老爹准备更豪华的生日礼物啦！
t_en: What are you talking about? Of course, I do! If the gems are expensive, Hook can buy a better birthday gift for daddy!

t_ch: 虎克不想单独把「菌菌」的眼睛留下来。如果拿到「菌菌」的小朋友发现他没有眼睛，肯定会很伤心吧？
t_en: I don't want to keep Junjun's eyes. If some kid gets a Junjun with no eyes, they'll be sad, right?

t_ch: 总之这是非常有用的情报！要不是你加入的时间太短，我都想把你提拔成鼹鼠党的三把手啦！
t_en: Anyhow, this is super useful intel! If you'd been with The Moles a bit longer, I'd be promoting you to third-in-command right now!

t_ch: 等娜塔莎姐姐把「菌菌」修好，我还会来找你帮忙的！你要随时响应漆黑的虎克大人的召唤哦！
t_en: I'll need your help again after Natasha fixes Junjun! Always be prepared to answer the call of Pitch-Dark Hook the Great!

t_ch: 还有什么事之后再说吧，你快去找找虎克，她应该还没有跑远。
t_en: Let's talk later. Go and find Hook, she can't have gone too far...

t_ch: 这孩子真是的，多说了她两句就跑得没影儿了。
t_en: *sigh* That girl... I'd barely gotten two sentences out.

t_ch: 被叫作「菌菌」的大熊玩偶
t_en: Junjun, the Cuddly Bear

t_ch: 会动？
t_en: Are they moving?

t_ch: 那就有点儿吓人了。
t_en: Ugh, please don't say that...

t_ch: 请把这样的意外叫作「生活的惊喜」。
t_en: There's no shortage of surprises in life.

t_ch: 你也太不小心了吧！
t_en: Were you distracted or something?

t_ch: 总之，这个玩偶一下就变成了昂贵的玩偶了，真是有些让人惊讶。
t_en: Well, our war torn bear here just became a priceless doll. Not bad, Junjun.

t_ch: 是你啊，{F#小姑娘}{M#小伙子}。我原本也想给你们送行，不过你们也看到了——这下层的破事，刚处理完一件，就又会冒出三件。
t_en: Ah, young {F#lady}{M#lad}. I wanted to see you off, but you know how things are around here in the underground. Solve one problem and three more take its place.

t_ch: 怎么，有事想问我吗？
t_en: Hm, something you wanted to ask?

t_ch: 你和娜塔莎的关系…
t_en: About you and Natasha...

t_ch: 娜塔莎信任我。在她看来，我这个前军官比她一个医生更适合做组织的领袖。
t_en: Natasha trusts me. In her eyes, an ex-military officer was more suited to be the leader of the organization than a doctor.

t_ch: 但我清楚真正的领袖应该具备什么。强大的内心，还有能团结众人的个人魅力——论这些素养，下层没人比得过她。
t_en: But the most important thing a leader needs is a strong heart. An ability to unite everyone toward a common cause. In that respect, no one in the underground comes close to her.

t_ch: 听说你以前来自上层。
t_en: I heard you came from the Overworld.

t_ch: 我以前其实也是银鬃铁卫的军官。负伤退伍以后，我跟着家里那口子来到下层，计划在这儿过上退休生活。
t_en: I was actually once an officer of the Silvermane Guards. After being discharged due to an injury, I followed my old lady here to the Underworld, where I planned to retire.

t_ch: 结果你也看见了，我现在每天琐事缠身，比在部队里的时候还更忙了。
t_en: But as things would have it, I ended up busier than ever.

t_ch: 后悔了吗？
t_en: Do you regret it?

t_ch: 没什么好后悔的！下层的生活固然辛苦，但周围的人们态度都很亲切，大家相处都像自家人一样融洽。
t_en: There is nothing to regret! Life in the Underworld is hard, but people here are kind and everyone is close like one big family.

t_ch: 怀念上层区的日子吗？
t_en: Do you miss the old days in the Overworld?

t_ch: 怀念？如果你指的是老战友之间的交情，那的确有不少值得怀念的地方。至于其它东西，我不觉得下层比上面差到哪儿去。
t_en: Do I miss it? If you're talking about the time with my comrades-in-arms, of course. But other than that, I don't really prefer it over life in the Underworld.

t_ch: 「地火」接下来会怎么样？
t_en: What's next for Wildfire?

t_ch: 「地火」已经撑过了最艰难的时候。有娜塔莎的领导，我们有信心带领下层民众走向更好的未来。
t_en: Wildfire has already made it through its darkest days. With Natasha's leadership, we have the confidence to lead the Underworlders to a brighter future.

t_ch: 这功劳大半要归你们。
t_en: And much of that is thanks to all of you.

t_ch: 没什么问题了。
t_en: I don't have any more questions.

t_ch: 幸好史瓦罗那确实藏着对你们有用的信息——差使你们忙前忙后这么久，最后要是还一无所获，那我可真要愧疚一阵了。哈哈哈！
t_en: Fortunately, Svarog turned out to have some useful information for you. After all your hard work, I'd feel quite guilty if you left empty-handed. Hahaha!

t_ch: 虽说你们的旅途远在天边，但我敢断言，咱们的道路总有一天会再交汇的。后会有期，「地火」的朋友！
t_en: While you still have a long journey ahead, I have no doubt our paths will cross again one day. Till we meet again, friends of Wildfire!

t_ch: 准备好了吗？要是再磨磨蹭蹭的话，我可就自己上去，不管你们了。
t_en: You ready yet? If you want to keep wasting time, I'll just go on ahead by myself.

t_ch: 怎么，还有什么话想说？那就快点。
t_en: What, still something you want to talk about? Alright, let's make it quick.

t_ch: 布洛妮娅有和你说什么吗？
t_en: Did Bronya say anything to you?

t_ch: 没说什么，就随便聊了几句。
t_en: Not much. We just chatted a bit.

t_ch: 具体讲讲。
t_en: Care to elaborate?

t_ch: …都说了没什么了。
t_en: ...It really isn't worth going into.

t_ch: 我答应她，要帮助她重建上下层之间的联系。就这样。
t_en: I promised her that I would help her rebuild the connection between the Overworld and the Underworld, that's all.

t_ch: 我看你心事重重的…
t_en: I can tell something's weighing on your mind...

t_ch: …有那么明显吗？
t_en: ...Is it that obvious?

t_ch: 我答应布洛妮娅，万一她出了什么事…我一定会亲自把她救出来。
t_en: I made a promise to Bronya that if something were to happen to her, I'd rescue her myself.

t_ch: 期待这段旅程吗？
t_en: Looking forward to this journey?

t_ch: 哼…没仔细想过。不过，跟你们一块旅行，的确常有意想不到的情况发生。
t_en: Hmm... I didn't really give it much thought. But traveling with you bunch does always lead to unexpected surprises.

t_ch: 酣畅的战斗和刺激的冒险…很不错，我不讨厌。
t_en: Hearty combat and exciting adventures... Not bad, I certainly don't hate it.

t_ch: 你和大家都道过别了？
t_en: Have you said goodbye to everyone?

t_ch: 当然。你是没看到约书亚的表情，那家伙差点就哭出来了。
t_en: Of course... Heh, you didn't see the look on Joshua's face. He could barely hold back his tears.

t_ch: 奥列格头儿…他没跟我说太多，但我知道他把担忧都藏在心里。
t_en: Chief Oleg... He didn't say much, but I know he's not the kind to wear his heart on his sleeve.

t_ch: 是我自己选择要上去的，娜塔莎也支持这个决定。你们肯定也需要个能打的帮手，不是吗？
t_en: It was my choice to go up, and Natasha supported my decision. Besides, you could use another person who can fight, right?

t_ch: 没有什么要问的了。
t_en: That's all for now.
